records.chapter: Endurance and divine recompense in grief
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: O’ Ash‘ath, if you grieve over your son, certainly it is the consequence of the blood relationship; but if you endure, then Allāh provides recompense for every affliction.
records.arabicText: يَا أَشْعَثُ، إِنْ تَحْزَنْ عَلَى ابْنِكَ فَقَدِ اسْتَحَقَّتْ مِنْكَ ذَلِكَ الرَّحِمُ، وَإِنْ تَصْبِرْ فَفِي اللهِ مِنْ كُلِّ مُصِيبَةٍ خَلَفٌ.

records.chapter: Endurance and divine recompense in grief
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: O’ Ash‘ath, if you endure even then matters will move on as ordained by Allāh but in that case you will deserve reward; while if you lose patience, matters will again move as ordained by Allāh, but in this case you will be bearing the burden (of sins).
records.arabicText: يَا أَشْعَثُ، إِنْ صَبَرْتَ جَرَى عَلَيْكَ الْقَدَرُ وَأَنْتَ مَأْجُورٌ، وَإِنْ جَزِعْتَ جَرَى عَلَيْكَ الْقَدَرُ وَأَنْتَ مَأْزُورٌ

records.chapter: Endurance and divine recompense in grief
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: O’ Ash‘ath, your son (when he lived) gave you happiness while, at the same time, he was a trial and hardship and (when he died) he grieved you while, at the same time, he has proved a source of reward and mercy for you.
records.arabicText: يَا أَشْعَثُ، ابْنُكَ سَرَّكَ وَهُوَ بَلاَءٌ وَفِتْنَةٌ، وَحَزَنَكَ وَهُوَ ثَوَابٌ وَرَحْمَةٌ.

records.chapter: Distance between East and West as sun's daily travel
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 294. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, was asked about the distance between East and West when he replied: One day’s travelling for the sun.
records.arabicText: ٢٩٤. وَقَدْ سُئِلَ عَنْ مَسَافَةِ مَا بَيْنَ الْمَشْرِقِ وَالْمَغْرِبِ فَقَالَ (عليه السلام): مَسِيرَةُ يَوْمٍ لِلشَّمْسِ.

records.chapter: Few learn from abundant lessons
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 297. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: How many are the objects of lessons, but how few the taking of lessons. [1]
records.arabicText: ٢٩٧. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَا أَكْثَرَ الْعِبَرَ وأَقَلَّ الِاعْتِبَارَ.

records.chapter: Seeking forgiveness through prayer after faults
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 299. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: I am not worried by a fault after which I get time to offer prayer in two units (rak‘ah) and beg safety from Allāh.
records.arabicText: ٢٩٩. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَا أَهَمَّنِي ذَنْبٌ أُمْهِلْتُ بَعْدَهُ حَتَّى أُصَلِّيَ رَكْعَتَيْنِ وَأسْأَلَ اللهَ الْعَافِيَة.

records.chapter: Messengers as interpreters and letters as true expressions
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 301. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Your messenger is the interpreter of your intelligence while your letter is more eloquent in expressing your true self.
records.arabicText: ٣٠١. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): رَسُولُكَ تَرْجُمَانُ عَقْلِكَ، وَكِتَابُكَ أَبْلَغُ مَا يَنْطِقُ عَنْكَ.

records.chapter: Self-respect prevents adultery
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 305. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: A self respecting man never commits adultery.
records.arabicText: ٣٠٥. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَا زَنَى غَيُورٌ قَطُّ.

records.chapter: Fatherly affection strengthens bonds between sons
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 308. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Mutual affection between fathers creates a relationship between the sons. Relationship is more in need of affection than affection is of relationship.
records.arabicText: ٣٠٨. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): مَوَدَّةُ الْآبَاءِ قَرَابَةٌ بَيْنَ الْأَبْنَاءِ، وَالْقَرَابَةُ إِلَى الْمَوَدَّةِ أَحْوَجُ مِنَ الْمَوَدَّةِ إِلَى الْقَرَابَةِ.

records.chapter: True faith requires greater trust in God than in oneself
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 310. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: The belief of a person cannot be regarded as true unless his trust in what is with Allāh is more than his trust in what he himself has.
records.arabicText: ٣١٠. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): لَا يَصْدُقُ إِيمَانُ عَبْدٍ، حَتّى يَكُونَ بِمَا فِي يَدِ اللهِ سُبْحَانَهُ أَوْثَقَ مِنهُ بِمَا فِي يَدِهِ.

records.chapter: Amīr al-mu’minīn’s warning to Anas ibn Mālik
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 311. When Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, came to Baṣrah he sent Anas ibn Mālik to Ṭalḥah and az-Zubayr to make them recall what he (Anas) himself had heard the Messenger of Allāh, peace be upon him and his descendants, say concerning them both, but he avoided doing so and when he came back to Amīr al-mu’minīn, he said that he had forgotten that matter.
records.arabicText: ٣١١. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام) لِأَنَسِ بْنِ مَالِكٍ، وَقَدْ كَانَ بَعَثَهُ إِلَى طَلْحَةَ وَالزُّبَيْرِ لَمَّا جَاءَ إِلَى الْبَصْرَةِ يُذَكِّرُهُمَا شَيْئاً مِمَّا سَمِعَهُ مِنْ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ (صلى الله عليه وآله) فِي مَعْنَاهُمَا، فَلَوَى عَنْ ذَلِكَ، فَرَجَعَ إِلَيْهِ، فَقَالَ إِنِّي أُنْسِيتُ ذَلِكَ الْأَمْرَ.

records.chapter: Amīr al-mu’minīn’s warning to Anas ibn Mālik
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: Thereupon, Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: If you are speaking a lie Allāh may afflict you with white spots (leucoderma) which even the turban may not cover.
records.arabicText: فَقال (عليه السلام): إِنْ كُنْتَ كَاذِباً فَضَرَبَكَ اللهُ بِهَا بَيْضَاءَ لَامِعَةً لَا تُوَارِيهَا الْعِمَامَةُ.

records.chapter: Amīr al-mu’minīn’s warning to Anas ibn Mālik
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: as-Sayyid ar-Raḍī says: White spot means leucoderma. After sometime this disease did occur to Anas’s face so much so that he was never seen without a veil. [1]
records.arabicText: قال السيد الرضى: يعني البرص، فأصاب أَنَساً هذا الداء فيما بعدُ في وجهه، فكان لا يُرى إلاّ مُبَرقعاً.

records.chapter: Responding to evil with evil
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 314. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Throw a stone in return from where one comes to you because evil can be met only with evil.
records.arabicText: ٣١٤. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): رُدُّوا الْحَجَرَمِنْ حَيْثُ جَاءَ، فَإِنَّ الشَّرَّ لَا يَدْفَعُهُ إِلَّا الشَّرُّ.

records.chapter: Leadership of believers versus wealth for the wicked
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 316. Amīr al-mu’minim, peace be upon him, said: I am the ya‘sūb (leader) of the believers, while wealth is the leader of the wicked.
records.arabicText: ٣١٦. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): أَنا يَعْسُوبُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ، وَالْمَالُ يَعْسُوبُ الْفُجَّارِ.

records.chapter: Leadership of believers versus wealth for the wicked
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: as-Sayyid ar-Raḍī says: It means that the believers follow me while the wicked follow wealth just as the bees follow their “ya‘sūb” who is their leader. [1]
records.arabicText: قال السيد الرضي: ومعنى ذلك أن المؤمنين يتبعونني، والفجار يتبعون المال، كما تتبع النحل يعسوبها، وهو رئيسها.

records.chapter: Amīr al-mu’minīn warns against destitution's spiritual and intellectual harms
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 319. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said to his son Muḥammad ibn al-Ḥanafiyyah: O’ my son, I fear lest destitution overtakes you. So, you should seek Allāh’s protection from it, because destitution is deficiency of religious belief, perplexity of intelligence, and it is conducive to hatred of obstinate people.
records.arabicText: ٣١٩. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام) لِابْنِهِ مُحَمَّدِ ابْنِ الْحَنَفِيَّةِ: يَا بُنَيَّ إِنِّى أَخَافُ عَلَيْكَ الْفَقْرَ فَاسْتَعِذْ بِاللَّهِ مِنْهُ فَإِنَّ الْفَقْرَ مَنْقَصَةٌ لِلدِّينِ مَدْهَشَةٌ لِلْعَقْلِ دَاعِيَةٌ لِلْمَقْتِ.

records.chapter: Leadership authority and advisory roles in governance
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 321. ‘Abdullāh ibn al-‘Abbās once advised Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, against his views, so he said: You have only to advise me but then I have to see (what to do); and if I act against your advice you have to follow me. [1]
records.arabicText: ۳۲۱. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام) لِعَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْعَبَّاسِ، وَقَدْ أَشَارَ عَلَيْهِ فِي شَيْ‏ءٍ لَمْ يُوَافِقْ رَأْيَهُ: لَكَ أَنْ تُشِيرَ عَلَيَّ وأَرَى، فَإِنْ عَصَيْتُكَ فَأَطِعْنِي.

records.chapter: Amīr al-mu’minīn on the deception of the Khārijites
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 323. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, passed by the dead bodies of the Khārijites on the day of the battle of Nahrawān and said: Woe unto you! You have been harmed by him who deceived you.
records.arabicText: ٣٢٣. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام) وَقَدْ مَرَّ بِقَتْلَى الْخَوَارِجِ يَوْمَ النَّهْرَوَانِ: بُؤْساً لَكُمْ، لَقَدْ ضَرَّكُمْ مَنْ غَرَّكُمْ.

records.chapter: Amīr al-mu’minīn on the deception of the Khārijites
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: He was asked: O’ Amīr al-mu’minīn, who deceived them? Then, he replied: Satan, the deceiver, and the inner spirit that leads one to evil deceived them through passions, made it easy for them to get into sins, promised them victory and eventually threw them into the Fire.
records.arabicText: فَقِيلَ لَهُ: مَنْ غَرَّهُمْ يَا أَمِيرَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ؟ فَقَالَ: الشَّيْطَانُ الْمُضِلُّ، وَالْأَنْفُسُ الْأَمَّارَةُ بِالسُّوءِ، غَرَّتْهُمْ بِالْأَمَانِيِّ، وَفَسَحَتْ لَهُمْ فِي المَعَاصِيِ، وَوَعَدَتْهُمُ الْإِظْهَارَ، فَاقْتَحَمَتْ بِهِمُ النَّارَ.

records.chapter: Age of accountability ends at sixty
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 326. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: The age up to which Allāh accepts any excuse for a human being is sixty years.
records.arabicText: ۳۲۶. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): الْعُمْرُ الَّذِي أَعْذَرَ اللهُ فِيهِ إِلَى ابْنِ آدَمَ سِتُّونَ سَنَةً.

records.chapter: Divine provision for the poor through the wealth of the rich
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 328. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Allāh, the Glorified, has fixed the livelihood of the destitute in the wealth of the rich. Consequently, whenever a destitute remains hungry it is because some rich person has denied (him his share). Allāh, the Sublime, will question them about it.
records.arabicText: ۳۲۸. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): إِنَّ اللهَ سُبْحَانَهُ فَرَضَ فِي أَمْوَالِ الْأَغْنِيَاءِ أَقْوَاتَ الْفُقَرَاءِ، فَمَا جَاعَ فَقِيرٌ إِلَّا بِمَا مَنَعَ بِهِ غَنِي، وَاللهُ تَعَالَى سَائِلُهُمْ عَنْ ذلِكَ.

records.chapter: Divine opportunity for the intelligent in others' shortcomings
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 331. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: When the disabled fall short in performing acts of obedience to Allāh, the Glorified, it is a good opportunity given by Allāh for the intelligent to perform such acts.
records.arabicText: ۳۳۱. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): إِنَّ اللهَ سُبْحَانَهُ جَعَلَ الطَّاعَةَ غَنِيمَةَ الْأَكْيَاسِ عِنْدَ تَفْرِيطِ الْعَجَزَةِ.

records.chapter: Amīr al-mu’minīn on property sharers and accidents
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 335. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: There are two sharers in the property of every person — successors and accidents.
records.arabicText: ۳۳۵. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): لِكُلِّ امْرِئٍ فِى مَالِهِ شَرِيكَانِ، الْوَارِثُ وَالْحَوَادِثُ.

records.chapter: Two types of knowledge: absorbed and merely heard
records.author: al-Sharīf al-Raḍī
records.englishText: 338. Amīr al-mu’minīn, peace be upon him, said: Knowledge is of two kinds — that which is absorbed and that which is just heard. The one that is heard does not give benefit unless it is absorbed.
records.arabicText: ٣٣٨. وَقَالَ (عليه ‏السلام): الْعِلْمُ عِلْمَانِ: مَطْبُوعٌ وَمَسْمُوعٌ وَلَا يَنْفَعُ الْمَسْمُوعُ إِذَا لَمْ يَكُنِ الْمَطْبُوعُ.
